title: Unidentified Kaleesh holy man

{{Quote|Our appeals have been ignored. It is now apparent that the Republic and the Jedi favor our enemies. General, you are our last hope against the Huk in this war. May the spirits of our ancestors watch over you and your troops.|The Kaleesh, to Grievous|<ref name="Eyes" />}}
A [[Kaleesh/Legends|Kaleesh]] '''holy man''' met with fellow Kaleesh [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]] prior to the [[General/Legends|general's]] departure to fight in the renewed [[Huk War]]. Grievous bowed before the Kaleesh at a landing site, where the latter anointed him with [[Color/Legends|red]] markings and called on their ancestors to watch over him.

==Biography==
{{Quote|As long as this old thing holds together, we'll be okay.|Grievous, to the Kaleesh|<ref name="Eyes" />}}
[[File:KaleeshHolyManWatchesMartyrDeparture-Visionaries.png|thumb|right|200px|The holy man watches Grievous depart]]
A holy [[Gender/Legends|man]]<ref name="Galaxy" /> of the [[Kaleesh/Legends|Kaleesh]] [[Species/Legends|species]] lived at the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the<ref name="Eyes" /> renewed<ref name="Unknown">{{InsiderCite|86|Unknown Soldier: The Story of General Grievous}}</ref> [[Huk War]] by [[29 BBY/Legends|29 BBY]].<ref name="Date">''[[Dark Lord: The Rise of Darth Vader]]'' states that [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous's]] [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] reconstruction as portrayed in "[[The Eyes of Revolution]]" had taken place a [[Standard decade/Legends|decade]] prior, and ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' places the novel in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]. Therefore, the renewal of the [[Huk War]] must have taken place by [[29 BBY/Legends|29 BBY]].</ref> After the [[Yam'rii/Legends|Yam'rii]], a species known derogatorily as the Huk by the Kaleesh, tarnished several sacred Kaleesh burial grounds on the [[Colonist/Legends|colony]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] [[Oben]], the Kaleesh [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]] returned to his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] [[Kalee/Legends|Kalee]] to prepare to take vengeance<ref name="Unknown" /> by attacking the Yam'rii on Oben.<ref name="Galaxy" /> At the sacred temple [[Shrupak]],<ref name="Endnotes">{{Blog|abelgpena|53|Endnotes for The Story of General Grievous, Part 1: The Unknown Soldier}}</ref> Grievous knelt before the holy man, who believed the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] had ignored Kaleesh appeals and favored the Yam'rii.<ref name="Eyes" />

The holy man called Grievous the "last hope" for their people, anointing the general by drawing [[Color/Legends|red]] markings across his face with a [[Limb/Legends|finger]]. The holy man blessed Grievous, calling for the spirits of their ancestors to watch over him and his [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]], to which the general retorted that he would be fine as long as his<ref name="Eyes" /> [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]], the ''[[Martyr]]'',<ref name="Lord of War">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/86/indexp2.html|text=The Story of General Grievous: Lord of War|int=The Story of General Grievous: Lord of War}}</ref> held together. The Kaleesh individual watched as Grievous took flight, although his voyage was cut short due to sabotage by the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], who wished to recruit Grievous.<ref name="Eyes" />

==Personality and traits==
The Kaleesh holy man had red skin and sharp white fingernails. They believed that the Republic and the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] showed favoritism towards the Yam'rii and that Grievous was the species' remaining hope for justice. The holy man called on Grievous' ancestors to bless him, and the general bowed before the Kaleesh to receive his blessing,<ref name="Eyes" /> even though Grievous was considered a [[God/Legends|demigod]] amongst his own people.<ref name="Lord of War" />

==Equipment==
At the time of the meeting with Grievous, the Kaleesh holy man held a white crooked [[Staff/Legends|staff]] and a brown satchel. The individual wore a white [[Robe/Legends|robe]], two white [[Bracelet/Legends|bracelets]] on each arm, and had a head adorned with an animal [[Bone/Legends|skull]].<ref name="Eyes" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Kaleesh holy man appeared in "[[The Eyes of Revolution]]," a [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] story written and illustrated by [[Warren Fu]] that was published in the anthology ''[[Star Wars: Visionaries]]'',<ref name="Eyes" /> released on [[April 2]], [[2005]].<ref name="Release">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54698/star_wars_visionaries_2005_1|text=Star Wars Visionaries (2005) #1}}</ref>
